summary: Iteration 2 CI-config fix verification: confirmed preview backend is still healthy after removing the explicit litellm pin from requirements.txt. /api/health=200, /api/changelog=200, litellm 1.80.0 still importable (transitive via emergentintegrations==0.1.2), and all 30 regression_smoke tests pass.

minor.issue: RequestsDependencyWarning: urllib3 (2.7.0) or chardet (7.4.3)/charset_normalizer (3.4.7) doesn't match supported version — cosmetic only, unchanged from prior iterations.
RuntimeWarning: coroutine 'close_litellm_async_clients' was never awaited at interpreter shutdown — upstream litellm issue, non-blocking.
litellm module exposes no __version__ attribute (pip show reports 1.80.0 correctly). Not a regression — same in iteration 39.

action_items: Re-run the GitHub Actions backend-tests.yml workflow to confirm the requirements.txt edit resolves the ResolutionImpossible error in CI (preview validation alone cannot prove this — the failure was pip-resolver-level, only reproducible on a fresh install).
critical_code_review_comments: requirements.txt line 68-73: the explicit litellm pin is commented out and a clear comment block documents why. Good — the transitive install from emergentintegrations==0.1.2 keeps litellm 1.80.0 pinned to the exact same wheel, so runtime behavior is unchanged.

Reliance on a transitive dep for a critical runtime library is a minor risk: if emergentintegrations bumps its litellm pin in a future release, the backend will silently follow. Consider re-adding an explicit `litellm==1.80.0` (version-only, NO URL/hash fragment) line if you want a defensive belt-and-suspenders pin. Not blocking.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 of a CI-config fix. Preview at localhost:8001 is healthy and 30/30 regression_smoke tests pass. litellm 1.80.0 is installed (pip show confirms) and importable via the transitive dependency from emergentintegrations==0.1.2. The actual ResolutionImpossible failure was pip-resolver-only and only manifests on a fresh install in GitHub Actions — preview validation here cannot prove the CI fix; the workflow must be re-run on GitHub to confirm.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause (per main agent context, validated here): requirements.txt previously had `litellm @ <url>#sha256=<hash>` while emergentintegrations==0.1.2's metadata pulls in litellm==1.80.0 as `litellm @ <same url>` (no hash). Pip's resolver treats `<url>` and `<url>#sha256=...` as two distinct specs for the same package, triggering ResolutionImpossible. Fix: commented out the explicit litellm pin in requirements.txt (line 68-73), letting emergentintegrations carry it transitively. Verified: pip show litellm => 1.80.0, `import litellm` succeeds, all 30 regression_smoke tests pass, /api/health and /api/changelog both 200.
